description: The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) is a world-leading centre for hydrography, delivering data and expertise to support safe, secure and thriving oceans. UKHO is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence .

details.body:
The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) is a world-leading centre for hydrography, delivering data and expertise to support safe, secure and thriving oceans.

UKHO is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UKHO announces the appointment of Mark Barclay as its new Non-Executive Chair, bringing extensive defence and aerospace expertise to the role.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UKHO appoints Mark Barclay as Non-Executive Chair

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A four‑week seabed mapping survey will bring together scientists from 11 organisations to improve understanding of the marine environment.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK scientists unite for seabed survey off the south‑west coast

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Fiona Bloor and Terry Thatcher from the UKHO have been recognised in the New Year Honours list for outstanding contributions to their respective fields.
ordered_featured_documents.title: UKHO specialists recognised in New Year Honours

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) is working with government partners to improve the UK’s resilience in satellite-based Positioning, Navigation and Timing (PNT).
ordered_featured_documents.title: UKHO supports UK resilience in positioning, navigation and timing

image.alt_text: Rear Admiral Angus Essenhigh presenting Captain (Aqeed) Ahmed Al Badi with the 2025 Alexander Dalrymple Award

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Head of the Oman National Hydrographic Office recognised for his contributions to hydrography by being awarded the 2025 Alexander Dalrymple Award
ordered_featured_documents.title: Captain (Aqeed) Ahmed Al Badi receives Alexander Dalrymple Award

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The UKHO’s Annual Report and Accounts for 2024 to 2025 is available to view and download
ordered_featured_documents.title: UK Hydrographic Office 2024–2025 Annual Report released

ordered_featured_links.title: UK Maritime Limits and Law of the Sea
UK Centre for Seabed Mapping (UK CSM)

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read our policy on Social media use. Find out About our services.

details.body: The board’s role is to support and constructively challenge the executive committee and to apply scrutiny both in the development of business strategies, cases, plans and targets; and in assessing our success in delivering the approved corporate plan.

role_appointments.title: Mark Barclay BSc (Hons), CEng, FIET, FAPM, FRAeS - Non-Executive Chair, UKHO

details.body:
The Chief Executive heads the Executive Committee and is responsible for the management of the trading fund.

Responsibilities include:

  • setting the direction of the organisation
  • defining and delivering the UKHO strategy and vision
  • leading the UKHO corporate and business plans, and transformation initiatives
  • ensuring alignment with MOD and wider UK government
  • implementing risk management policies
  • maintaining the reputation of the UKHO
  • developing and leading the Executive Leadership team

role_appointments.title: Vanessa Blake - Interim Chief Executive, UKHO

details.body:
The Chief Customer Officer has overall responsibility for the customer-focused commercial strategy.

Responsibilities include:

  • leading the customer-focused commercial strategy
  • delivering the UKHO’s sales revenue and gross margin targets
  • acting as the lead ‘Customer Champion’, ensuring customers remain at the centre of all activities
  • contributing to overall strategic management

role_appointments.title: Vanessa Blake - Chief Customer Officer, UKHO

details.body:
The Chief Digital and Technology Officer has overall responsibility for the digital and data strategy.

Responsibilities include:

  • leading the digital, data and cyber strategies
  • delivering the digital capabilities the organisation needs to meet its customers needs
  • responsible for the cyber and information security managing digital risk
  • leading on strategic innovation of digital services
  • responsible for AI governance and Assurance

role_appointments.title: Alex Bowen - Transformation Director & Interim Chief Technology Officer
Amy Carrillo - Chief People Officer

details.body:
The Interim Chief Customer and People Officer responsibilities include:

  • leading the people strategy 
  • delivering the UKHO’s recruitment strategy and talent management 
  • leading initiatives to define the UKHO’s organisational culture 
  • leading the customer-focused commercial strategy and Admiralty sales revenue
  • acting as the lead ‘Customer Champion’, ensuring customers remain at the centre of all activities 
  • contributing to overall strategic management

role.title: Interim Chief Customer and People Officer

role_appointments.title: Amy Carrillo - Interim Chief Customer and People Officer

details.body:
The UK National Hydrographer is responsible for:

  • Maintaining international relations, engagement, data acquisition, technical standards and capacity building
  • Law of the Sea
  • Intellectual Property and Legal

role_appointments.title: RAdm Angus Essenhigh OBE - National Hydrographer and Deputy Chief Executive

details.body:
The Director of Data Acquisition and Defence has overall responsibility for the UKHO’s national and international data supply network and our relationship with the Defence customer.

Responsibilities include:

  • leading the national and international partnership programme
  • delivering the required data and permissions to support our role as hydrographic office and the delivery of UKHO products and services to Defence
  • leading the engagement strategy with the wider hydrographic community and UK government
  • contributing to overall strategic management

role.title: Director of Data Acquisition​ and Defence

role_appointments.title: RAdm Angus Essenhigh OBE - Director of Data Acquisition​
David English - Director of Finance & Corporate Services

details.body:
The Chief Finance Officer is responsible for Dstl’s Finance, Strategy and Governance Division.

The purpose of this Division is to manage and oversee all aspects of financial management within Dstl, to develop Dstl’s corporate strategies and plans, to maintain and operate the governance framework within Dstl, including Dstl’s approach to risk management, to oversee Dstl’s corporate performance regime, and to deliver a complete intellectual property management service for Dstl.

role_appointments.title: David English - Chief Finance Officer

details.body: The Chief Operating Officer is responsible for leading a range of Dstl’s core business enabling operations which include health, safety and environmental protection, facilities and infrastructure (including the National Biosecurity Centre Programme), technical services, data and digital services, security and resilience.

role_appointments.title: David English - Chief Operating Officer

details.body:
The Finance Director:

  • provides strategic financial advice to underpin strategic aims and policies
  • defines robust, rigorous controls for effective financial management which provides return on investment

role_appointments.title: David English - Finance Director, Dstl

details.body:
The Chief Operating Officer has overall responsibility for the operations division.

Responsibilities include:

  • leading the operations division
  • leading the delivery of UKHO’s products and services
  • leading and delivering the UKHO change programme to transform the organisation
  • contributing to overall strategic management

role_appointments.title: David Tomaney - Chief Operating Officer

details.body: The role of SLC’s Board is to set the strategic direction of the company within the policies and business framework set by ministers and oversee the effective and efficient discharge of its statutory responsibilities.

details.description: The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) takes the security of our systems seriously. If you believe you have found a vulnerability on any UKHO system, you can report it using the guidance and policy provided.
details.title: Report a vulnerability on an UKHO system
